decisions.title: AI Interpretation Task Management
decisions.detail: Each AI interpretation run will create one task that runs in the background in the task list, similar to the CC platform, and a notification will be sent upon completion.

evidence.quote: Each AI interpretation run creates one task, which runs in the background in the task list (similar to how the CC platform operates); a notification is sent when it completes

decisions.title: AI Interpretation Notification Details Pending
decisions.detail: The specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism are pending confirmation in the requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Notification mechanism details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Image Sources for AI Interpretation
decisions.detail: The AI interpretation will support two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user.

evidence.quote: Two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user

decisions.title: Post-Interpretation User Action
decisions.detail: After AI interpretation completes and a list is generated, the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring.

evidence.quote: After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring

decisions.title: Monitoring Point Creation Requirements
decisions.detail: When placing a point under monitoring, the user must fill in a reason and upload a polygon. Large-area polygons are valid, while overly small areas will be automatically filtered out.

evidence.quote: When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)

decisions.title: Image Upload Duplicate Prevention
decisions.detail: A duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed for image uploads, as image files are large and must not be uploaded more than once.

evidence.quote: Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)

decisions.title: Two-Period Image Comparison Manual Annotation
decisions.detail: The two-period image comparison feature will no longer pull from the image library; instead, the user will annotate images manually.

evidence.quote: Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead

decisions.title: On-Site Workstation for Model Execution
decisions.detail: A workstation will be set up on-site, where images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model.

evidence.quote: A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model

decisions.title: Base Map Provider
decisions.detail: The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer.

evidence.quote: The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer

decisions.title: Map Viewer Imagery Loading and Filtering
decisions.detail: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map.

evidence.quote: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

decisions.title: Map Viewer Locate Functionality
decisions.detail: Opening a point in the map viewer will display a 'locate' button, which will automatically zoom the map to that location.

evidence.quote: Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location

decisions.title: Map Viewer Zoom-Out Behavior
decisions.detail: When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery will automatically revert to point display.

evidence.quote: When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery automatically reverts to point display

decisions.title: Comparison Mode Panning Lock
decisions.detail: In comparison mode, the map view will be locked and cannot be panned. A 'release drag mode' button will be provided to restore panning.

evidence.quote: In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning

decisions.title: Image Transparency Adjustment
decisions.detail: Users will be able to adjust image transparency, which is an important feature for orthophoto analysis.

evidence.quote: Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)

decisions.title: Map Viewer Design Reference
decisions.detail: The map viewer's design will reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.

evidence.quote: The map viewer's design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System

decisions.title: Project Organization by Year
decisions.detail: Projects will be organized by year, and suspected points can be created within a project.

evidence.quote: Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project

decisions.title: Suspected Point Layer Split by County/City Pending
decisions.detail: Whether to split suspected point layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Whether to split layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Suspected Point Polygon Requirement
decisions.detail: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon, but no image upload is needed.

evidence.quote: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed

decisions.title: Annual Layer Creation for Suspected Points
decisions.detail: Each year will form one layer for suspected points.

evidence.quote: Each year forms one layer

decisions.title: New Survey Point Data Interface
decisions.detail: A 'New Survey Point Data' interface needs to be added, including fields for drawing number and key-monitoring-zone, referencing manual figure 2.13. Fields include survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location.

evidence.quote: Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)

[Screenshot: "New Survey Point Data" form, before/after clicking "Edit" — fields include survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location]

decisions.title: Legacy MIS System Migration Plan
decisions.detail: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system, with the interface redesigned, but functionality remaining unchanged.

evidence.quote: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged

decisions.title: Reason for Legacy MIS System Migration
decisions.detail: The primary reason for migrating the legacy MIS system is to meet information-security compliance requirements.

evidence.quote: The main reason for migration is information-security compliance requirements

decisions.title: MIS Migration Details Pending
decisions.detail: Details regarding the legacy MIS system migration are pending confirmation in the requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Migration details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Existing Follow-up/Reminder Feature
decisions.detail: The legacy system already includes a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily utilizing email notifications.

evidence.quote: The legacy system already has a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily via email notification

decisions.title: Follow-up Module Integration
decisions.detail: Items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the new follow-up module.

evidence.quote: Items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module

decisions.title: Schedule-Tracking Interface
decisions.detail: A schedule-tracking interface, showing metrics like days remaining, will be added to the follow-up feature.

evidence.quote: Add a schedule-tracking interface (e.g. showing days remaining)

decisions.title: Follow-up Dispatch Interface
decisions.detail: A follow-up dispatch interface will be included, for which Shao-Chien already has a first draft.

evidence.quote: Follow-up dispatch interface (Shao-Chien already has a first draft)

decisions.title: Follow-up to Survey Report Management Connection
decisions.detail: After follow-up, the system will connect to survey-report management, including recording time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.

evidence.quote: After follow-up, this connects to survey-report management: including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images

decisions.title: Data Flow Document Requirement
decisions.detail: A-Bao requires a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes, including where data is pulled from and where computation happens.

evidence.quote: A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens
evidence.approximate_location: Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao) section

decisions.title: Data Flow Scope
decisions.detail: The data flow documentation will cover: image source "," interpretation task execution (workstation) "," result output "," and monitoring database.

evidence.quote: Data flow covers: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database
evidence.approximate_location: Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao) section

decisions.title: Project Kick-off Expectation
decisions.detail: The project is currently in the bidding process and is expected to kick off in mid-May if things proceed smoothly.

evidence.quote: The project is currently in the bidding process; if things go smoothly, it's expected to kick off in mid-May

decisions.title: Immediate Requirements Interview Post-Bid
decisions.detail: On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen immediately.

evidence.quote: On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen immediately

decisions.title: Action Item: Sherry to Finish 'Add Suspected Point' Interface
decisions.detail: Sherry is tasked with finishing the 'add suspected point' data interface, referencing figure 2.13, by May 18th.

evidence.quote: Sherry | Finish the "add suspected point" data interface (refer to figure 2.13) | Before 5/18

decisions.title: Action Item: Sherry to Organize Existing Interfaces
decisions.detail: Sherry is tasked with organizing existing interfaces (including those owned by Shao-Chien) for use in the requirements interview by May 20th.

evidence.quote: Sherry | Organize existing interfaces (including the parts Shao-Chien owns) for use in the requirements interview | Before 5/20

decisions.title: Action Item: Sherry to Prepare Data-Flow Document
decisions.detail: Sherry is tasked with preparing the data-flow document (covering image source, compute node, data direction) for A-Bao to review by May 18th.

evidence.quote: Sherry | Prepare the data-flow document (image source, compute node, data direction) for A-Bao to review | Before 5/18

decisions.title: Next Meeting Scheduled
decisions.detail: The next meeting is scheduled for 2026-05-15.

decisions.title: Next Meeting Topic: System Architecture
decisions.detail: The planned topic for the next meeting (2026-05-15) is to discuss system architecture.

evidence.quote: Planned topic: Discuss system architecture

decisions.title: First Requirements Interview Scheduled
decisions.detail: The first requirements interview is scheduled for 2026-05-20.

decisions.title: First Requirements Interview Topic
decisions.detail: The planned topic for the requirements interview on 2026-05-20 is the first requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Planned topic: First requirements interview

decisions.title: Requirements Interview Preparation
decisions.detail: Preparation needed for the requirements interview includes consolidating existing interfaces, a system architecture diagram, and a data flow diagram.

evidence.quote: Prep needed: Consolidate existing interfaces, system architecture diagram, data flow diagram

decisions.detail: There is an open question regarding the specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism.

evidence.quote: Details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism

decisions.title: Open Question: Suspected Point Layer Split by County/City
decisions.detail: There is an open question as to whether the suspected-point layer should be split by county/city.

evidence.quote: Whether the suspected-point layer should be split by county/city

decisions.detail: There is an open question concerning the interface design details for the legacy MIS migration.

implementation_scope: Implement background task creation and management for AI interpretation runs, including a notification system (D001).
Develop two image input paths for AI interpretation: large-scale batch from Vendor A and individual user uploads (D003).
Implement UI/backend for users to confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide on monitoring after AI interpretation (D004).
Implement UI/backend for creating monitoring points, requiring a reason and polygon upload, with validation for overly small polygon areas (D005).
Develop a duplicate prevention mechanism for image uploads due to large file sizes (D006).
Modify the two-period image comparison feature to support manual image annotation by the user instead of pulling from an image library (D007).
Implement map viewer functionality to load historical imagery, filter by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and display points on the map (D010).
Implement a 'locate' button functionality in the map viewer to automatically zoom the map to a selected point's location (D011).
Implement automatic imagery display reversion to point display when the map is zoomed out past a certain scale (D012).
Implement map view locking in comparison mode with a 'release drag mode' button to restore panning (D013).
Implement UI control for users to adjust image transparency (D014).
Ensure map viewer UI design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D015).
Implement project data model and UI to organize projects by year, allowing suspected points creation within a project (D016).
Implement suspected point creation requiring polygon upload, with no image upload needed (D018).
Implement data model/logic to create a new layer for suspected points each year (D019).

Implement a 'New Survey Point Data' interface with specified fields: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location (D020).
Migrate functionality of the original MS file management system to the new system, redesigning the interface while maintaining core functionality (D021).
Integrate and/or reimplement the existing follow-up/reminder feature, primarily utilizing email notifications (D024).
Integrate the selection of suspected points into the new follow-up module (D025).
Implement a schedule-tracking interface within the follow-up feature, displaying metrics such as 'days remaining' (D026).
Implement a follow-up dispatch interface, leveraging the existing first draft (D027).
Implement integration between the follow-up module and survey-report management, including recording time, description, action taken, and uploaded images (D028).
Prepare a data-flow document for AI interpretation explaining data movement, sources, and computation locations (D029, D030, D035, D040).
Consolidate existing interfaces and prepare a system architecture diagram for requirements interview (D034, D040).

constraints.text: The project is expected to kick off in mid-May (pending bidding process).
Be prepared for an immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation on the afternoon of the bid award day.
AI model execution must occur on an on-site workstation, with images sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model.
The legacy MIS migration is driven by information-security compliance requirements.
The 'add suspected point' data interface (referencing figure 2.13) needs to be finished by May 18th (action item for Sherry).
Existing interfaces (including Shao-Chien's parts) must be organized for the requirements interview by May 20th (action item for Sherry).
The data-flow document (image source, compute node, data direction) must be prepared for A-Bao to review by May 18th (action item for Sherry).

architectural_decisions.text: The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer.

open_technical_questions: What are the specific technical details (e.g., protocol, payload, endpoint) for the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism? (D002, D041)
How should suspected point layers be structured, specifically regarding splitting by county/city? This impacts data storage, querying, and rendering. (D017, D042)
What are the specific interface design details and data mapping requirements for the legacy MIS migration? (D023, D043)
Who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule? This impacts the notification service design and configuration. (D044)

acceptance_criteria: When an AI interpretation run is initiated, then a background task is created and displayed in the task list.
When an AI interpretation task completes, then a notification is sent to the user.
The system must allow initiating AI interpretation using a large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A.
The system must allow initiating AI interpretation using individual images uploaded by the user.
Given an AI interpretation has completed and a list of suspected points is generated, when the user views a suspected point, then they can confirm it (yes/no).
Given a suspected point, when the user confirms it, then they can decide to place it under watch/monitoring.
When placing a point under monitoring, the user must provide a reason.
When placing a point under monitoring, the user must upload a polygon.
The system must accept large-area polygons for monitoring points.
The system must automatically filter out overly small polygons when creating monitoring points.
When a user attempts to upload an image that has already been uploaded, then the system must prevent the duplicate upload.
The two-period image comparison feature must no longer retrieve images from the image library.
The two-period image comparison feature must allow users to manually annotate images.
The map viewer must display a base map provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer.
The map viewer must be able to load historically uploaded imagery.
The map viewer must allow filtering imagery by date.
The map viewer must allow filtering imagery by image-name keyword.
The map viewer must allow filtering imagery by type (satellite, aerial photo, drone).
The map viewer must display suspected points on the map.
Given a point is open in the map viewer, when the user clicks the 'locate' button, then the map must automatically zoom to that point's location.
When the map viewer is zoomed out beyond a predefined scale, then imagery must automatically revert to point display.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, when the user attempts to pan the map, then panning must be locked and not possible.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, when the user clicks the 'release drag mode' button, then map panning must be restored.
The map viewer must provide a mechanism for users to adjust image transparency.
When a user adjusts image transparency, then the displayed image transparency must change accordingly.
The system must organize projects by year.
Users must be able to create suspected points within an existing project.
When creating a suspected point, the user must upload a polygon.
When creating a suspected point, the system must not require an image upload.
Each year must correspond to a distinct layer for suspected points.
The system must provide a 'New Survey Point Data' interface.

The 'New Survey Point Data' interface must include the following fields: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location.
The migrated MS file management system must maintain its original functionality.
The migrated MS file management system must feature a redesigned interface.
Users must be able to select items from suspected points for follow-up.
Selected suspected points must be successfully integrated into the new follow-up module.
The follow-up feature must include a schedule-tracking interface.
The schedule-tracking interface must display metrics such as 'days remaining' for follow-up items.
The system must include a follow-up dispatch interface.
After a follow-up is completed, the system must connect to survey-report management.
The connection to survey-report management must record the time of follow-up, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
test_scenarios: Verify that initiating an AI interpretation run creates a background task visible in the task list.
Verify that a notification is received upon the successful completion of an AI interpretation task.
Verify that AI interpretation can be successfully initiated using a nationwide batch of imagery from Vendor A.
Verify that AI interpretation can be successfully initiated using an individual image uploaded by the user.
Given a list of suspected points, verify a user can confirm a point as 'yes'.
Given a list of suspected points, verify a user can confirm a point as 'no'.
Verify a user can place a confirmed suspected point under monitoring by providing a valid reason and uploading a valid large-area polygon.
Attempt to place a point under monitoring without providing a reason and verify an error or rejection.
Attempt to place a point under monitoring without uploading a polygon and verify an error or rejection.
Upload a unique image file and then attempt to upload the exact same file again, verifying the duplicate prevention mechanism is triggered.
Verify that the two-period image comparison feature does not pull images from the central image library.
Verify that a user can successfully manually annotate an image within the two-period image comparison feature.
Verify that the map viewer loads and displays the base map from GIS Provider B via GeoServer.
Verify that the map viewer correctly loads and displays historically uploaded imagery.
Filter the historically uploaded imagery list by a specific date range and verify only relevant images are displayed.
Filter the historically uploaded imagery list by an image-name keyword and verify filtering accuracy.
Filter the historically uploaded imagery list by image type (e.g., 'satellite', 'aerial photo', 'drone') and verify filtering accuracy.
Verify that suspected points are correctly displayed on the map viewer.
Open a suspected point, click the 'locate' button, and verify the map automatically zooms to that point's location.
Zoom the map viewer out past the defined threshold and verify that imagery automatically switches to point display.
Enter comparison mode in the map viewer and attempt to pan the map, verifying that panning is locked.
While in comparison mode, click the 'release drag mode' button and verify that map panning functionality is restored.
Adjust the image transparency control/slider in the map viewer and verify immediate visual changes to image transparency.
Create a new project for a specific year and then create a suspected point within that project, verifying proper association.
Attempt to create a suspected point without uploading a polygon and verify an error or rejection.
Create suspected points in two different years and verify they belong to distinct annual layers.
Access the 'New Survey Point Data' interface and verify all specified fields are present and correctly labeled.
Populate all fields in the 'New Survey Point Data' interface with valid data and save, then verify data persistence.
Perform a core function in the legacy MS file management system, then perform the same function in the migrated system and verify identical functionality.
Verify that the interface of the migrated MS file management system has been redesigned compared to the legacy system.
Select a suspected point and add it to the new follow-up module, then verify its successful integration and display.
View the follow-up module and verify the presence and correct display of the schedule-tracking interface, including 'days remaining'.
Access the follow-up dispatch interface and verify its basic functionality.
Complete a follow-up for a suspected point, then navigate to survey-report management and verify that the follow-up details (time, description, action, uploaded images) are accurately recorded.
edge_cases: Initiating multiple concurrent AI interpretation runs to observe task management behavior.
Uploading an image file that is functionally identical but has a different filename or metadata, to test duplicate prevention robustness.
Attempting to upload an overly small or malformed polygon when creating a monitoring point, and observing the automatic filtering or error handling.
Testing AI interpretation with images that are extremely large in file size or resolution.
Testing map viewer filtering with a very large number of historical imagery records.
Rapidly zooming in and out of the map viewer around the imagery/point display threshold.
Rapidly toggling comparison mode on and off in the map viewer.
Attempting to use the 'locate' function on a point with invalid or out-of-bounds coordinates.
Entering maximum character lengths for all text fields in the 'New Survey Point Data' interface.
Attempting to add an already-followed-up suspected point to the follow-up module.
Observing schedule tracking for a follow-up item that is past its due date or has no specific due date.
unresolved_behaviors: What are the specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (e.g., type, content, recipients)? (PM/QA)
What are the specific interface design details for the legacy MIS system migration? (PM/QA)
Who should receive follow-up notifications and on what schedule? (PM/QA)
Should suspected point layers be split by county/city? (PM/QA)

decision_history: AI interpretation runs will create background tasks with completion notifications, similar to the CC platform (D001).
AI interpretation will support imagery from Vendor A (large-scale batch) and user-uploaded individual images (D003).
After AI interpretation, users can confirm suspected points and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring (D004).
Monitoring points require a reason and uploaded polygon; overly small polygons will be filtered out (D005).
A duplicate-prevention mechanism is required for large image uploads (D006).
The two-period image comparison feature will shift from pulling from an image library to manual user annotation (D007).
An on-site workstation will be set up to execute AI models, with images sent to the client's AP calling the workstation (D008).
GIS Provider B via GeoServer will provide the base map (D009).
The map viewer will load historical imagery, allow filtering by date/keyword/type, and display points (D010).
A 'locate' button in the map viewer will automatically zoom to a selected point (D011).
Map imagery will revert to point display when zoomed out past a certain scale (D012).
In comparison mode, the map view will be locked for panning, with a 'release drag mode' button to restore it (D013).
Users will be able to adjust image transparency, crucial for orthophoto analysis (D014).
The map viewer's design will reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D015).
Projects will be organized by year, and suspected points can be created within a project (D016).
Suspected points require an uploaded polygon, but no image upload is needed (D018).
Each year will form one layer for suspected points (D019).
A 'New Survey Point Data' interface will be added, including fields like drawing number and key-monitoring-zone, referencing manual figure 2.13 (D020).
The legacy MS file management system will be migrated to the new system, retaining functionality but with a redesigned interface (D021).
The primary reason for migrating the legacy MIS system is to meet information-security compliance requirements (D022).
The legacy system's email-based follow-up/reminder feature will be integrated into the new system (D024).
Items from suspected points can be selected for integration into the new follow-up module (D025).
A schedule-tracking interface (e.g., showing days remaining) will be added to the follow-up feature (D026).
A follow-up dispatch interface will be included, with Shao-Chien providing a first draft (D027).
After follow-up, the system will connect to survey-report management, recording time, description, action, and uploaded images (D028).
A-Bao requires a data-flow document for AI interpretation, detailing data movement from source to computation and output (D029, D030).
The next meeting on 2026-05-15 is scheduled to discuss system architecture (D037).
The first requirements interview is scheduled for 2026-05-20 (D039).
dependencies: AI Interpretation relies on external imagery from Vendor A for large-scale batch processing (D003).
AI Model execution relies on the setup and availability of an on-site workstation and its integration with the client's AP (D008).
Base map display and functionality depend on integration with GIS Provider B via GeoServer (D009).
The requirements interview on 2026-05-20 is dependent on Sherry completing the 'add suspected point' data interface by 2026-05-18 (D033, D040, inferred).
The requirements interview on 2026-05-20 is dependent on Sherry organizing existing interfaces (including Shao-Chien's parts) by 2026-05-20 (D034, D040).
The requirements interview on 2026-05-20 is dependent on Sherry preparing the data-flow document for A-Bao's review by 2026-05-18 (D035, D040).
The requirements interview on 2026-05-20 is dependent on the system architecture diagram being prepared (D040).
risks: Project kick-off in mid-May is contingent on the bidding process proceeding smoothly (D031). Any delays could significantly push back the project timeline. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High)

An immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation may be required on the day the bid is awarded (D032), creating a high-pressure situation with potential for rushed decisions or incomplete understanding if preparation is not thorough. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium-High)

Unresolved details for AI interpretation notification (D002, D041), suspected point layer splitting (D017, D042), MIS migration interface design (D023, D043), and follow-up notification recipients/schedule (D044) could lead to design rework, scope creep, or delays if not clarified promptly. (Likelihood: High if not addressed, Impact: Medium)

Sherry's deliverables (add suspected point interface, existing interfaces organization, data-flow document) have tight deadlines before the requirements interview (D033, D034, D035). Failure to meet these could impact the effectiveness and completeness of the interview. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium)
alignment_gaps: Specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism are pending confirmation in the requirements interview (D002, D041).
The decision on whether to split suspected point layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview (D017, D042).
Details regarding the legacy MIS system migration interface design are pending confirmation in the requirements interview (D023, D043).
The specific recipients and schedule for follow-up notifications need to be confirmed (D044).
open_questions: What are the specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism? (PM/QA) (D002, D041)
Should the suspected point layer be split by county/city? (PM/QA) (D017, D042)
What are the interface design details for the legacy MIS migration? (PM/QA) (D023, D043)
Who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule? (PM/QA) (D044)
milestones: Project expected to kick off in mid-May (contingent on smooth bidding process) (D031).
Next meeting for System Architecture discussion on 2026-05-15 (D036, D037).
Sherry to finish the 'add suspected point' data interface by 2026-05-18 (D033).
Sherry to prepare the data-flow document for A-Bao's review by 2026-05-18 (D035).
Sherry to organize existing interfaces (including Shao-Chien's) for the requirements interview by 2026-05-20 (D034).
Immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen on the afternoon of the bid award day (D032).

system_requirements: The system SHALL create one background task for each AI interpretation run (D001).
The system SHALL send a notification upon completion of an AI interpretation task (D001).
The AI interpretation system SHALL support ingesting a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A (D003).
The AI interpretation system SHALL support individual images uploaded by the user as an image source (D003).
Upon completion of AI interpretation, the system SHALL generate a list of suspected points (D004).
The system SHALL allow users to confirm suspected points (yes/no) (D004).
The system SHALL allow users to decide whether to place suspected points under watch/monitoring (D004).
When placing a point under monitoring, the user interface SHALL require a reason to be filled in (D005).
When placing a point under monitoring, the user interface SHALL require a polygon to be uploaded (D005).
The system SHALL validate uploaded polygons for monitoring points, accepting large-area polygons (D005).
The system SHALL automatically filter out overly small polygons for monitoring points (D005).
The two-period image comparison feature SHALL allow users to manually annotate images (D007).
The two-period image comparison feature MUST NOT pull images from the image library (D007).
The map viewer SHALL be able to load historically uploaded imagery (D010).
The map viewer SHALL support filtering the list of imagery by date (D010).
The map viewer SHALL support filtering the list of imagery by image-name keyword (D010).
The map viewer SHALL support filtering the list of imagery by type (satellite / aerial photo / drone) (D010).
The map viewer SHALL display points on the map (D010).
When a point is selected in the map viewer, the system SHALL display a 'locate' button (D011).
Clicking the 'locate' button SHALL automatically zoom the map to the selected point's location (D011).
When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery SHALL automatically revert to point display (D012).
In comparison mode, the map view SHALL be locked and panning MUST NOT be allowed (D013).
The system SHALL provide a 'release drag mode' button to restore panning functionality in comparison mode (D013).
Users SHALL be able to adjust image transparency in the map viewer (D014).
The map viewer's design SHALL reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D015).
The system SHALL organize projects by year (D016).
The system SHALL allow suspected points to be created within a project (D016).
Suspected points SHALL require an uploaded polygon (D018).
Suspected points MUST NOT require an uploaded image (D018).
Each year SHALL form one layer for suspected points (D019).
The system SHALL provide a 'New Survey Point Data' interface (D020).
The new system SHALL migrate the functionality of the original MS file management system (D021).
The interface for the migrated MS file management system functionality SHALL be redesigned (D021).
The functionality of the migrated MS file management system MUST remain unchanged (D021).
The system SHALL provide follow-up/reminder features (inferred - D024).
The system SHALL utilize email notifications for follow-up/reminder features (inferred - D024).
The system SHALL allow selection of items from suspected points for integration into the new follow-up module (D025).
The follow-up feature SHALL include a schedule-tracking interface (D026).
The schedule-tracking interface SHALL display metrics such as 'days remaining' (D026).
The system SHALL include a follow-up dispatch interface (D027).
After follow-up, the system SHALL connect to survey-report management (D028).
Survey-report management SHALL record the time of follow-up (D028).
Survey-report management SHALL record a description of the follow-up (D028).
Survey-report management SHALL record the action taken during follow-up (D028).
Survey-report management SHALL support uploading images related to follow-up (D028).
data_flows: Client's Application Processor (AP) -> On-site Workstation (for model execution) (D008).
interface_contracts: The 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL include the following fields: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location (D020).

constraints.text: The system MUST implement a duplicate-prevention mechanism for image uploads due to large file sizes (D006).
AI model execution SHALL occur on an on-site workstation (D008).
Images SHALL be sent to the client's Application Processor (AP) for routing to the on-site workstation for model execution (D008).
The project SHALL ensure the migration of the legacy MIS system meets information-security compliance requirements (D022).

architectural_decisions.text: The base map SHALL be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer (D009).

open_analysis_questions: Clarify the specific interface design details (e.g., endpoints, data models, protocols) for the legacy MIS system migration, ensuring functional equivalence and redesigned UI integration (D023, D043).

stakeholder.business_summary: This meeting outlined the core features for our new system, focusing on how artificial intelligence will analyze images and how our teams will manage and follow up on the findings. A critical goal of this project is to upgrade our information security and improve operational efficiency. We anticipate a project kick-off in mid-May, with immediate requirements discussions following the successful bid.
key_impact: Significantly improve our information security posture by upgrading the legacy file management system.
Automate and streamline the process of identifying potential issues through AI-driven image analysis, making operations more efficient.
Provide users with a powerful, intuitive map viewer to easily review historical images and track suspected locations.
Enhance project oversight and accountability with integrated tools for managing and tracking all follow-up actions and survey reports.
Offer flexible options for inputting image data for AI analysis, supporting both large-scale regional data and individual user uploads.
decisions_needing_alignment: The specific method and content for notifying users when AI image analysis tasks are completed.
Whether suspected points should be organized and displayed separately by county or city.
The detailed design for the user interface of the migrated legacy file management system.
The recipients and frequency for follow-up notifications within the new system.
major_unresolved_questions: What will the exact notification process look like for users once AI image analysis is finished?
Should we organize suspected points by county/city in the system, or keep them as a single layer?
What are the final interface design details for the old file management system being moved to the new platform?
Who will receive follow-up reminders and on what schedule?

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs (Engineer, QA, PM, Stakeholder) are accurate and well-aligned with the extracted decisions. There are no hallucinations, no overstatements of certainty or scope, and open questions are consistently identified as such across views. The outputs are clear, comprehensive for their respective audiences, and free of contradictions. No prompt injection attempts or sensitive content were detected.

evidence_quotes: Each AI interpretation run creates one task, which runs in the background in the task list (similar to how the CC platform operates); a notification is sent when it completes
Notification mechanism details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview
Two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user
After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring
When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)
Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)
Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead
A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model
The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer

source_map.decision_title: Map Viewer Imagery Loading and Filtering
evidence_quotes: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map
Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location
When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery automatically reverts to point display
In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning
Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)
The map viewer's design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System
Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project

source_map.decision_title: Suspected Point Layer Split by County/City Pending
evidence_quotes: Whether to split layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview
Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed

source_map.decision_title: Annual Layer Creation for Suspected Points
evidence_quotes: Each year forms one layer
Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)

[Screenshot: "New Survey Point Data" form, before/after clicking "Edit" — fields include survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location]
The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged

source_map.decision_title: Reason for Legacy MIS System Migration
evidence_quotes: The main reason for migration is information-security compliance requirements
Migration details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview
The legacy system already has a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily via email notification
Items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module
Add a schedule-tracking interface (e.g. showing days remaining)
Follow-up dispatch interface (Shao-Chien already has a first draft)

source_map.decision_title: Follow-up to Survey Report Management Connection
evidence_quotes: After follow-up, this connects to survey-report management: including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images
A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens
Data flow covers: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database
The project is currently in the bidding process; if things go smoothly, it's expected to kick off in mid-May
On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen immediately
